user_query:
I need a new reusable skill that encodes our team’s Technical Spec Review process so it can be triggered consistently and evaluated over time. Please read the materials under input/ to capture intent and success criteria, then produce the following deliverables:

1) Create a skill folder at output/spec-reviewer/ with an SKILL.md that has:
- YAML frontmatter with a non-generic name and a pushy description that clearly states what the skill does and when it should trigger (include concrete trigger contexts and phrasing variations).
- A body that includes these sections: Intent Capture, Interview Questions & Edge Cases, Step-by-Step Instructions (imperative voice), Output Format (provide a report template the model must follow), Examples (at least one input/output pair), Test Case Design Guidance, and a section titled "Draft Assertions" listing at least 3 objectively checkable checks we can later automate (e.g., presence of required headings, word count ranges, checklist completion).

2) Create test prompts at output/spec-reviewer/evals/evals.json with 2 or 3 realistic evals that reflect the process in input/test_prompts.json. Use this structure:
{
  "skill_name": "<must exactly match SKILL.md name>",
  "evals": [
    {"id": 1, "prompt": "...", "expected_output": "what success looks like in prose", "files": []},
    {"id": 2, ...}
  ]
}
Keep assertions empty here; we’ll add them later.

3) For each eval in evals.json, create a corresponding metadata file at output/spec-reviewer-workspace/iteration-1/<eval-slug>/eval_metadata.json with:
{
  "eval_id": <number>,
  "eval_name": "kebab-case descriptive name",
  "prompt": "the same prompt text",
  "assertions": []
}
Use a sensible slug for <eval-slug> derived from eval_name. Do not run anything — just author these metadata files so we can wire them into a runner later.

4) Create 20 trigger evaluation queries at output/trigger_evals.json as an array of objects [{"query": "...", "should_trigger": true|false}]. Include 10 should_trigger and 10 should_not_trigger items that are realistic, detailed, and representative of how engineers would ask for spec reviews (mix formal/casual phrasings, near-misses for negatives). No duplicates.

Constraints:
- Read only from input/ and write only to output/.
- Keep everything in English.
- Don’t assume access to any external tools or browsers.
- Make the description sufficiently "pushy" so the skill actually triggers when it should, per our org’s needs.

When done, list what you created and any open questions you’d want answered before running a first evaluation round.
